Member-only story

11 min read
Just now

--

OpenClaw just shipped parallel-free — a no-account, no-API-key web search provider built on Parallel's MCP. Here's how it installs, what it actually returns, and how it compares to a Tavily free-tier baseline.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Table of Contents

• Why This Matters

• What parallel-free Actually Is

• How OpenClaw Wires It Up

• Installation and First Call

• Inspecting the Wire Protocol

• Search Result Quality vs Tavily Free Tier

• When To Pick Which

• Operational Caveats

• Takeaways
1. Why This Matters
Every developer building AI agents in 2026 hits the same wall on day three: the model needs real-time web information, and every search API on the market wants an account, a credit card, and a key in your env file.
The pain compounds when you’re prototyping. You don’t know yet whether you need news filtering, domain whitelists, AI-generated summaries, or just “tell me what…
